ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: How often in the past month did you talk with your friends about drinking alcohol?
Notes: 0=Never; 1=Once or twice; 2=3-4 times; 3=5-6 times; 4=7-9 times; 5=10 or more times

ElementDescription: How often in the past month did you talk with your friends about reducing risks related to drinking alcohol?
Notes: 0=Never; 1=Once or twice; 2=3-4 times; 3=5-6 times; 4=7-9 times; 5=10 or more times

ElementDescription: how often in the past month did you talk with your friends about staying safe when drinking alcohol?
Notes: 0=Never; 1=Once or twice; 2=3-4 times; 3=5-6 times; 4=7-9 times; 5=10 or more times

ElementDescription: How often in the past month did you talk to a friend about drinking less alcohol?
Notes: 0=0 times; 1=1 time; 2=2 times; 3= 3 or more times

ElementDescription: How often in the past month did you encourage a friend to drink less alcohol?
Notes: 0=0 times; 1=1 time; 2=2 times; 3= 3 or more times

ElementDescription: How often in the past month did you support a friend who did not want to drink?
Notes: 0=0 times; 1=1 time; 2=2 times; 3= 3 or more times

ElementDescription: How often in the past month did you talk with a friend before going out about how to avoid drinking too much?
Notes: 0=0 times; 1=1 time; 2=2 times; 3= 3 or more times

ElementDescription: How often do you normally talk with your friends about drinking alcohol?
Notes: 0=Never; 1=Seldom; 2=At least once a week; 3=Twice a week; 4=A few times a week; 5=Nearly everyday

ElementDescription: How often do you normally talk with your friends about reducing risks related to drinking alcohol?
Notes: 0=Never; 1=Seldom; 2=At least once a week; 3=Twice a week; 4=A few times a week; 5=Nearly everyday

ElementDescription: How often do you normally talk with your friends about staying safe when drinking alcohol?
Notes: 0=Never; 1=Seldom; 2=At least once a week; 3=Twice a week; 4=A few times a week; 5=Nearly everyday

ElementDescription: My partner was more attentive to me when I was drinking
Notes: 0 = Never; 1 = 1-2 Times a year; 2 = 3-6 Times a year; 3 = Every 1-2 months; 4 = 1-3 times a month; 5 = Once a week or more

ElementDescription: With your partner, how often do you: Remind yourself that their drinking is not your fault
Notes: 0 = Never; 1 = 1-2 Times a year; 2 = 3-6 Times a year; 3 = Every 1-2 months; 4 = 1-3 times a month; 5 = Once a week or more

ElementDescription: With your partner, how often do you: Wait until they are sober to talk to him/her when a problem comes up that requires you to discuss it

ElementDescription: I can recognize when would be a good time to open the discussion

ElementDescription: I can say the right things in order to make this conversation as constructive as possible

ElementDescription: I can say the right things when it's the appropriate time to say them
